questions.q: In which field of Indian cinema did Naushad (1919-2006) earn his fame?
questions.explanation: Naushad was a celebrated film music director associated with the Golden Age of Hindi cinema.

questions.q: In which Indian city was the composer Naushad born in 1919?
questions.explanation: Naushad Ali was born on 25 December 1919 in Lucknow, Uttar Pradesh.

questions.q: For which prestigious lifetime honour was the music director Naushad recognised in 1981?
questions.explanation: Naushad received the Dadasaheb Phalke Award, India's highest honour in cinema, for 1981.

questions.q: Which 1960 historical epic featured an acclaimed musical score by Naushad?
questions.explanation: Naushad composed the music for K. Asif's grand historical epic Mughal-e-Azam, released in 1960.

questions.q: The composer Naushad was closely associated with shaping many defining songs of which legendary playback singer?
questions.explanation: Naushad helped shape many of Mohammed Rafi's defining songs across the 1950s and 1960s.

questions.q: Naushad's 1952 classic Baiju Bawra was built heavily around which musical tradition?
questions.explanation: Baiju Bawra (1952) was rooted in Hindustani classical ragas, marking a landmark in Naushad's career.

questions.q: In which decade did Naushad begin establishing himself as a leading film music director?
questions.explanation: Naushad rose to prominence as a film composer during the 1940s, with Rattan (1944) as a key breakthrough.

questions.q: Naushad is most remembered for his contribution to which industry's films?
questions.explanation: Naushad was a towering music director of Hindi (Bollywood) cinema.

questions.q: Which classical Indian instrument is famously associated with the kind of music Naushad championed?
questions.explanation: Naushad drew on classical instruments like the sitar in integrating Hindustani classical idioms into film scores.

questions.q: Naushad is often described as a leading composer of which era of Indian cinema?
questions.explanation: Naushad is regarded as one of the greatest composers of the Golden Age of Hindi cinema.

questions.q: Which 1957 epic, directed by Mehboob Khan and starring Nargis, had its memorable score composed by Naushad?
questions.explanation: Naushad scored Mehboob Khan's Mother India (1957), the iconic film led by Nargis.

questions.q: The famous Mughal-e-Azam song 'Mohe Panghat Pe' and other numbers were composed by Naushad in which year of the film's release?
questions.explanation: Mughal-e-Azam, with Naushad's celebrated songs, was released in 1960.

questions.q: For the classical song 'Prem Jogan Ban Ke' in Mughal-e-Azam, Naushad notably recruited which classical vocalist?
questions.explanation: Naushad had the great khayal vocalist Ustad Bade Ghulam Ali Khan sing classical pieces in Mughal-e-Azam.

questions.q: Which 1949 film, starring Dilip Kumar, Raj Kapoor and Nargis, featured Naushad's music and the hit 'Tu Kahe Agar'?
questions.explanation: Naushad composed the music for Mehboob Khan's Andaz (1949), a major hit of the period.

questions.q: Naushad enjoyed a celebrated long-term partnership with which lyricist on films like Baiju Bawra, Mother India and Mughal-e-Azam?
questions.explanation: Naushad's most enduring collaboration was with lyricist Shakeel Badayuni, who wrote the songs for Baiju Bawra, Mother India and Mughal-e-Azam.

questions.q: The Baiju Bawra song 'Man Tarpat Hari Darshan Ko Aaj', composed by Naushad, was famously sung by which singer?
questions.explanation: Mohammed Rafi sang the raga-based devotional 'Man Tarpat Hari Darshan Ko Aaj' for Naushad in Baiju Bawra.

questions.q: Naushad's score for Baiju Bawra won the very first Filmfare Award in which category?
questions.explanation: Naushad won the first-ever Filmfare Best Music Director award, for Baiju Bawra, at the 1st Filmfare Awards in 1954.

questions.q: Naushad frequently collaborated with which director on landmark films such as Andaz, Aan and Mother India?
questions.explanation: Naushad had a long, productive partnership with director Mehboob Khan.

questions.q: Which 1952 Mehboob Khan film, one of India's first in Technicolor and scored by Naushad, starred Dilip Kumar and Nadira?
questions.explanation: Naushad scored Aan (1952), among the first Indian films presented in Technicolor, marking Nadira's debut.

questions.q: In which decade did the veteran composer Naushad pass away?
questions.explanation: Naushad died in 2006 in Mumbai, in the 2000s decade.

questions.q: Naushad was known for integrating which type of regional rural music into his film compositions, alongside classical ragas?
questions.explanation: Naushad blended folk idioms, especially from the Uttar Pradesh region, with Hindustani classical ragas.

questions.q: Which 1955 Dilip Kumar starrer, scored by Naushad, featured the Mohammed Rafi song 'O Door Ke Musafir'?
questions.explanation: Naushad composed the music for Uran Khatola (1955), starring Dilip Kumar, including 'O Door Ke Musafir' sung by Mohammed Rafi.

questions.q: Which 1944 film is widely credited as the breakthrough that established Naushad as a top-rank film music director?
questions.explanation: Naushad's score for Rattan (1944) was a huge success that cemented his reputation.

questions.q: Naushad composed the songs for which 1946 film featuring the legendary singer K. L. Saigal, including 'Jab Dil Hi Toot Gaya'?
questions.explanation: Naushad composed the music for Shahjehan (1946), which featured K. L. Saigal singing 'Jab Dil Hi Toot Gaya'.

questions.q: For the Naushad-composed song 'Madhuban Mein Radhika Nache Re' in Kohinoor (1960), Dilip Kumar learned to play which instrument for the picturisation?
questions.explanation: Dilip Kumar trained on the sitar for the picturisation of Naushad's classical song 'Madhuban Mein Radhika Nache Re' in Kohinoor (1960).

questions.q: Naushad collaborated with the singer Suraiya in several 1940s films, including which 1946 musical hit?
questions.explanation: Naushad's Anmol Ghadi (1946) prominently featured singer-actress Suraiya alongside Noor Jehan.

questions.q: Late in his career, Naushad composed music for which 2005 film based on the life of a Mughal-era figure?
options: Taj Mahal: An Eternal Love Story
questions.explanation: One of Naushad's final works was the score for Taj Mahal: An Eternal Love Story (2005), directed by Akbar Khan.

questions.q: For the colour version of Mughal-e-Azam released in 2004, Naushad's original soundtrack was re-recorded and which song remained a centrepiece?
options: Aaj Phir Jeene Ki Tamanna Hai
questions.explanation: 'Pyar Kiya To Darna Kya', composed by Naushad, remained the centrepiece of Mughal-e-Azam's music.

questions.q: The Mughal-e-Azam song 'Pyar Kiya To Darna Kya', composed by Naushad, was sung by which playback singer?
questions.explanation: Lata Mangeshkar sang Naushad's iconic 'Pyar Kiya To Darna Kya' in Mughal-e-Azam (1960).

questions.q: Naushad is famous for using large orchestras and grand arrangements; for which film did he employ an exceptionally large orchestra to achieve a lavish classical sound?
questions.explanation: Naushad used an exceptionally large orchestra for Mughal-e-Azam to match the film's grand scale.
